title: Takeda — Overview
html:
Takeda Account Overview

Governing Thought

Takeda is ZoomRx's most diversified client engagement — 11 active projects spanning 5+ therapeutic areas (GI, Rare Disease, Neuroscience, Dermatology/Immunology, Hematology) with an unusually broad methodological toolkit that includes PET, Conference Studies, Color Preference RTs, Radiographic RTs, Global Pulse, Medical Data Assessments, and an AI Workshop. Unlike accounts where ZoomRx is primarily a PET vendor, Takeda uses ZoomRx as a research swiss-army-knife, pulling from the full service catalog to address problems that range from pre-launch market shaping (Entyvio VERDICT) to novel research formats (Color Preference RT for mezagitamab and zasocitinib). The partnership, dating to approximately 2019-2020, has steadily expanded from single-brand GI tracking (Gattex PET, Motegrity PET) into a multi-TA, multi-methodology engagement. Two dynamics define the next 12 months: (1) the Entyvio biosimilar cliff (first biosimilar expected 2025-2026), which will reshape Takeda's largest franchise and create urgent competitive tracking demand; and (2) the zasocitinib pipeline (TYK2 inhibitor, Phase 3 in psoriasis/PsA/IBD), which represents Takeda's growth bet across immunology and could become a full launch suite opportunity for ZoomRx.

The Takeda Landscape

At a Glance

Dimension Detail
AO [Populate]
AD [Populate]
FY25 Base [Populate from Kimble]
FY26 Target [Populate from Kimble]
HQ Tokyo, Japan (US operations: Cambridge, MA)
CEO Costa Saroukos (designated CEO effective 2025; succeeding Christophe Weber)
Revenue (FY2024, ending Mar 2025) ~$28-29B [UNVERIFIED]
Therapeutic Areas GI, Rare Disease, Neuroscience, Oncology, Plasma-Derived Therapies, Immunology/Dermatology
Active TAs with ZoomRx GI (Entyvio, Eohilia, Gattex), Rare Disease (Takhzyro, HAE), Neuroscience (Narcolepsy), Derm/Immunology (Zasocitinib/PsO), Hematology (Mezagitamab)
Active project count 11
Active project types PET, Conference Studies, Color Preference RT, Radiographic RT, Global Pulse, Medical Data Assessment, AI Workshop, Prelaunch Research
Partnership tenure ~6-7 years (since ~2019-2020)
Vinoth's role [Populate — owned or overseen]

Company Context

Takeda is a large, diversified, Japan-headquartered pharmaceutical company with US operations based in Cambridge, MA. Under long-tenured CEO Christophe Weber (2015-2025), the company executed the transformative Shire acquisition (2019), which built out the Rare Disease and Plasma-Derived Therapies portfolios. Costa Saroukos, formerly CFO, was designated as the successor CEO effective 2025.

The Entyvio cliff is the defining corporate event. Entyvio (vedolizumab, IBD) is Takeda's largest franchise at approximately $6-7B globally [UNVERIFIED]. The first biosimilar is expected in the 2025-2026 timeframe, creating the single largest revenue risk in Takeda's portfolio. Takeda's response is two-pronged:

  1. Entyvio lifecycle management: The VERDICT prelaunch study (Project #1, Apr 2026) signals Takeda is preparing a next-generation Entyvio or indication expansion play to defend the franchise.
  2. Zasocitinib as the growth replacement: TAK-279 (zasocitinib), a TYK2 inhibitor in Phase 3 across psoriasis, PsA, and IBD, is Takeda's largest pipeline bet. If successful, it replaces Entyvio's declining revenue with a new-mechanism franchise across immunology and potentially GI.

R&D investment: Approximately 15-17% of revenue [UNVERIFIED], with pipeline concentrated in GI/Immunology, Rare Disease, and Neuroscience.

Portfolio by Therapeutic Area

GI (Core Franchise)

Brand Mechanism Indication Revenue Notes
Entyvio (vedolizumab) Anti-integrin (gut-selective) IBD (UC + Crohn's) ~$6-7B global [UNVERIFIED] Largest brand; biosimilar threat 2025-2026; VERDICT prelaunch study active
Eohilia (budesonide oral suspension) Corticosteroid (topical GI) EoE (Eosinophilic Esophagitis) [Populate] Specialty GI; active PET at ZoomRx (Rare GI PET 2025-26)
Gattex (teduglutide) GLP-2 analog Short Bowel Syndrome (SBS) [Populate] Historical PET (2020, 2024, 2024-25) + ATU/PCA; mature engagement

Rare Disease

Brand Mechanism Indication Revenue Notes
Takhzyro (lanadelumab) Anti-kallikrein (plasma) HAE (Hereditary Angioedema) [Populate] HAE Medical Data Assessment (Oct 2025); switching topline delivered
Adzynma (ADAMTS13) Replacement enzyme TTP (Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura) [Populate] First replacement ADAMTS13; launched 2023
Livtencity (maribavir) UL97 kinase inhibitor CMV (refractory/resistant) [Populate] Treatment-refractory niche; competes in different space vs Prevymis (prophylaxis)

Neuroscience

Brand Mechanism Indication Revenue Notes
Narcolepsy pipeline [Multiple] Narcolepsy (Type 1 & 2) Pipeline Conference Studies active: World Sleep Congress 2025, SLEEP 2025, AAN 2026
Soticlestat Cholesterol 24-hydroxylase inhibitor Epilepsy (Dravet, Lennox-Gastaut) Pipeline Historical Epilepsy PET (2021); if approved, creates launch tracking opportunity
Vyvanse (lisdexamfetamine) CNS stimulant ADHD Declining (generic competition) Historical RT (2020); no active ZoomRx work

Immunology / Dermatology

Brand Mechanism Indication Revenue Notes
Zasocitinib (TAK-279) TYK2 inhibitor Psoriasis, PsA, IBD (Phase 3) Pipeline Color Preference RT (Feb 2026) + Global Pulse (Nov 2025); Takeda's biggest pipeline bet

Hematology

Brand Mechanism Indication Revenue Notes
Mezagitamab Anti-CD38 IgA Nephropathy Pipeline Color Preference RT (Apr 2026); pre-launch research

Pipeline to Watch

Asset Mechanism Stage Why It Matters for ZoomRx
Zasocitinib (TAK-279) TYK2 inhibitor Phase 3 (PsO, PsA, IBD) Full launch suite opportunity: PET, ATU, Message Testing, Launch KPI, HCP-Pt, MSL tracking. Multi-indication = multi-project. Competes with Bristol-Myers' deucravacitinib (Sotyktu) and potentially AbbVie/J&J JAK pipeline.
Mezagitamab Anti-CD38 Phase 3 (IgAN) Pre-launch research already active (Color Preference RT). If approved, IgA Nephropathy launch tracking across nephrology is a greenfield market.
Soticlestat CH24H inhibitor Phase 3 (Epilepsy) Historical ZoomRx engagement (Epilepsy PET 2021). If approved, re-entry to neurology tracking.
Frexalimab Anti-CD40L Phase 3 (MS) Acquired via Sanofi partnership. Multiple sclerosis is a large, competitive TA; if approved, creates a new TA entry point for ZoomRx.
Entyvio SC Anti-integrin (subcutaneous) Approved (lifecycle) Subcutaneous formulation as biosimilar defense — similar to Merck's QLEX strategy. Could drive SubQ adoption tracking work.

ZoomRx Service Footprint

Service GI (Entyvio) GI (Eohilia/Gattex) Rare Disease (Takhzyro/HAE) Neuro (Narcolepsy) Derm/Imm (Zasocitinib) Heme (Mezagitamab)
PET X (Eohilia/Gattex)
Conference Study X (3 conferences) X (AAN 2026)
Color Preference RT X X
Radiographic RT X (PsO)
Global Pulse X
Medical Data Assessment X (HAE)
Prelaunch (VERDICT) X
AI Workshop X (cross-TA)
ATU X (Gattex historical)
HCP-Patient
Message Testing
MSL / MEE
Digital Tracking
Launch KPI

Key takeaway: The service footprint is broad (8+ distinct project types) but shallow per TA — most TAs have only 1-2 active project types. The massive white space is in core tracking services (PET, ATU, Message Testing, MSL) across brands that currently only receive novel/specialized methodologies. Zasocitinib alone could fill 6+ service cells upon launch.

Competitive Landscape Summary

Takeda Corporate Competitive Positioning

Franchise Primary Competitors Competitive Dynamic
Entyvio (IBD) Humira/biosimilars (AbbVie), Stelara (J&J, LOE), Skyrizi + Rinvoq (AbbVie), Tremfya (J&J) Entyvio has safety/gut-selectivity edge but biosimilar cliff approaching. AbbVie's Skyrizi/Rinvoq tandem is the commercial threat.
Zasocitinib (Derm/Imm) Sotyktu/deucravacitinib (BMS, TYK2), Skyrizi (AbbVie, IL-23), Tremfya (J&J, IL-23), Rinvoq (AbbVie, JAK) TYK2 class is emerging; zasocitinib enters Phase 3 behind Sotyktu but with broader indication strategy (PsO + PsA + IBD).
Takhzyro (HAE) Cinryze, Haegarda (CSL Behring), Orladeyo (BioCryst), Lanadelumab biosimilars (future) Takhzyro is market leader in HAE prophylaxis. Oral alternatives (Orladeyo) create convenience competition.
Eohilia (EoE) Dupixent (Sanofi/Regeneron — approved for EoE), generic budesonide (off-label) Dupixent is the primary competitor in EoE; biologic vs topical steroid positioning.
Narcolepsy (pipeline) Xywav/Xyrem (Jazz), Wakix (Harmony), Sunosi (Axsome) Narcolepsy is a concentrated market with Jazz dominant. Any Takeda entry needs differentiation.
Mezagitamab (IgAN) Filspari/sparsentan (Travere), Nefecon (Calliditas/Stada) IgA Nephropathy is an emerging market with multiple new entrants. Early launch positioning is critical.

Vendor Competition at Takeda

Area Likely Incumbent ZoomRx Position
PET [Unknown — populate from SOW intel] Established via Gattex/Eohilia; no confirmed PET on flagship brands (Entyvio, Takhzyro)
Conference Studies [Unknown] Active and recurring (3 narcolepsy conferences, AAN 2026)
Color Preference / Radiographic RT Likely limited vendor competition (niche) ZoomRx appears to be the primary provider
Medical Data Assessment [Unknown] Active on HAE
AI / Innovation [Unknown] AI Workshop (Oct 2025) signals Takeda interest in ZoomRx's AI capabilities

SWOT Analysis

Strengths

  • Methodology diversity: 8+ distinct project types delivered — demonstrates ZoomRx's versatility beyond PET tracking
  • Multi-TA footprint: Active across 5+ TAs — any single TA budget cut does not jeopardize the account
  • Novel format expertise: Color Preference RT, Radiographic RT, Conference Studies — ZoomRx is positioned as the partner for non-standard research needs
  • Pipeline positioning: Already engaged on zasocitinib (2 projects) and mezagitamab (1 project) before launch — early relationship building
  • AI Workshop signal: The Oct 2025 AI Workshop indicates Takeda is interested in ZoomRx's innovation capabilities, not just data collection

Weaknesses

  • Shallow per-TA engagement: Most TAs have only 1-2 project types — no deep, multi-service franchise engagement exists yet
  • No PET on flagship brands: Entyvio and Takhzyro — the two largest revenue brands — do not have active PET trackers at ZoomRx
  • Contact map is sparse: No documented relationship intelligence from SOWs or call notes (pending enrichment)
  • No recurring tracker revenue: Unlike J&J or Merck where PET provides predictable annual revenue, most Takeda projects are one-off or episodic
  • Financial visibility is limited: No AO Plan data, no revenue breakout, no pipeline value — the account is under-documented financially

Opportunities

  • Zasocitinib full launch suite: Phase 3 readouts could trigger PET, ATU, Message Testing, Launch KPI, HCP-Pt, MSL tracking — potentially $300-500K+ in new work across indications
  • Entyvio biosimilar defense tracking: Competitive PET, ATU, or patient switching studies as biosimilar enters — $100-200K opportunity
  • MSL/MEE across all TAs: Takeda has no MSL tracking at ZoomRx despite operating across 5+ TAs — each TA is a potential MSL engagement
  • Mezagitamab IgAN launch: If Phase 3 succeeds, pre-launch and launch tracking in nephrology is a new market for ZoomRx
  • Conference Study expansion: ZoomRx is already the conference study partner for neuroscience — expand to GI conferences (DDW, ACG), dermatology (AAD), and rare disease meetings
  • AI Workshop follow-through: Convert the Oct 2025 AI Workshop signal into a recurring AI-enabled service offering (Dynamic Dialogues, AI-powered analysis)

Threats

  • Entyvio revenue decline: If biosimilar erosion is severe, Takeda may cut GI research budgets across the board — affecting Eohilia and pipeline GI work
  • Zasocitinib clinical risk: Phase 3 failure in any indication could eliminate the largest growth opportunity on the account
  • Contact fragmentation: Takeda US has ~5,000 employees; insights teams span multiple BUs. Without a mapped contact strategy, relationship-building is inefficient.
  • Vendor consolidation: Takeda may consolidate MR vendors as part of cost discipline — ZoomRx's diverse but shallow engagement may not be "deep enough" to survive a vendor rationalization
  • Japan-HQ decision-making: Some research decisions may require Japan-HQ approval, adding complexity and cycle time to the sales process

What the Account Team Needs to Know (Fast Orientation)

  1. This is not a PET account — it is a methodology account. Takeda values ZoomRx for versatility across project types, not for depth within one methodology. Every pitch should lead with "we can do X, Y, and Z for you" rather than "here is our PET."

  2. Zasocitinib is the BD story. The TYK2 inhibitor in Phase 3 across psoriasis, PsA, and IBD is Takeda's growth bet. If Phase 3 succeeds, the launch suite (PET + ATU + Message Testing + Launch KPI + HCP-Pt + MSL) across 3 indications could be the single largest new business opportunity on the account.

  3. Entyvio biosimilar creates urgency. The account team should be prepared with a biosimilar defense research package (competitive PET, patient switching, message testing for differentiation) before Takeda's GI team starts looking for it.

  4. The AI Workshop was a signal. The Oct 2025 AI Workshop with transcripts and an offsite presentation signals that Takeda is interested in ZoomRx's AI capabilities at a strategic level. This is an above-brand entry point that should be cultivated.

  5. Conference Studies are the recurring engine. Three narcolepsy conferences plus AAN 2026 demonstrate a pattern: Takeda uses ZoomRx for pre/post conference research. Expanding this to GI, derm, and rare disease conferences is the most natural growth path within existing service types.

  6. The contact map needs investment. Unlike J&J or Merck where deep relationship intelligence exists, the Takeda contact map is largely unpopulated. SOWs, proposals, and call notes from active projects are the enrichment sources. This is a priority for the next 30 days.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
